top_signals.title: Russia Scrambles for Asian Jet Fuel as Crisis Deepens
top_signals.summary: Russia's fuel crisis has reached the point where one of the world's biggest oil producers is preparing to import jet fuel. According to Reuters, Russia is set to receive at least 200,000 barrels of jet fuel originating in Japan through a complex chain of traders and ship-to-ship transfers via South Korea, underscoring just how badly Ukrainian drone strikes have disrupted the country's refining system. The cargo is expected to load from Chiba, Japan, in the first half of July before heading to South Korea's Yeosu port for a ship-to-ship transfer.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: ‘Markets find their footing’: Bitcoin holds $61,000 rebound ahead of US Independence Day as soft jobs data eases rate fears
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin holds above $61,000 and ether above $1,700 as soft U.S. jobs data eases Fed hike bets and spot ETFs snap a 10-day outflow streak.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Credit Agricole Increases BPM Stake, Boosting Position in Italy
top_signals.summary: The Credit Agricole SA Campus in Paris, France, on Monday, Feb. 2, 2026. The euro-area economy grew more than expected at the end of last year, demonstrating resilience to the trade turmoil unleashed by Donald Trump.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.summary: Clean power purchase agreements, favoured by companies like Google and Meta, expected to rise sharply as Biden-era support ends
